title: RAT v3 plugin manifest (rat/1)
description: The manifest contract for a RAT v3 plugin. One of the three coupled artifacts of the contract triple (manifest + proto + capability URI). Operator-editable; designed for IDE autocomplete + inline validation per ADR-002 D3. This is the kind-agnostic ENVELOPE schema: it validates the common structure for every axis. Per-kind constraints on provides (e.g. 'a kind:format must provide rat://format/v1/scan') are layered as separate per-kind schemas — see contracts/schema/kinds/<kind>.v1.json + ADR-011. FROZEN at v1 (ADR-011, rat/1.5): additive backward-compatible changes only within rat/1; anything breaking ships as plugin.v2.json under api_version rat/2.
$comment: STATUS (2026-06-01): the orchestrating CORE IS NOT BUILT YET (Phase 1). Enforcement this schema and the axis protos describe in the present tense — `compatible_core` checking, `provides`/`requires` (C5) capability enforcement, identity/tenant (C2/C7) checks, audit emission — is the contract the core MUST implement; it does NOT run today. The wire contracts + 32 reference plugins are real and frozen (rat/1..rat/1.4); the core is designed, not running. `make conformance` tests references against golden vectors — it does NOT verify a live deployment, and manifest `provides`/`conformed_capabilities` are currently self-asserted (no enforcer exists yet). See roadmap/current.md + reviews/08.

api_version.description: The contract major this manifest targets. Mirrors K8s apiVersion. Only the contract major is encoded (ADR-002 D4).

kind.description: The plugin axis. The v1 starting set is the 16+ open-set axes (ADR-001). Community axes are allowed via the open `kind` pattern, but unknown kinds SHOULD be proposed via ADR before publishing. The enum lists the v1 blessed axes for autocomplete; the pattern keeps the set open.

name.description: Globally-unique plugin name. Convention: rat-<kind>-<impl> (e.g. rat-format-deltalake).

version.description: Plugin version, SemVer. Governs the PLUGIN's own lifecycle. Distinct from capability major-versioning (ADR-002 D4), which is encoded in the capability URI, not here.

support_url.description: Where users file issues for THIS plugin. Load-bearing for the support-attribution model: rat diagnose points blame here when a failure originates in this plugin (reviews/02 Stage 8). Required for team+ marketplace listing; recommended always.

compatible_core.description: The core contract majors this plugin is known to work against. A CHECKED compatibility gate (like VSCode engines.vscode), not advisory (reviews/02 Stage 10). Defaults to [the api_version major] when omitted.

provides.description: Capabilities this plugin implements. The registry indexes these so peers can resolve capability -> provider WITHOUT naming this plugin. C5: what is declared here is what the gateway enforces at runtime — a plugin may only serve calls for capabilities it provides.

requires.description: Capabilities this plugin needs, by capability (never by peer plugin name). The reconciler refuses to install a plugin whose requires are unsatisfied. Capability-not-implementation negotiation is the core coupling mechanism (ADR-001).

suggests.description: Capabilities that enhance this plugin but are not required. Informational; the reconciler does not block on these.

contributes.description: Extension slots this plugin contributes into (e.g. a UI panel into the portal). The target is a capability-shaped slot URI; the contribution is named by component.

metadata_schema.description: Relative path to a JSON Schema describing this plugin's own configuration block (operator-supplied config). Enables IDE validation of plugin config, same mechanism as the manifest itself.

resources.description: C4: resource asks + limits, K8s-shaped, MANDATORY. Even in-process solo plugins declare them so the reconciler can reason about placement and the operator can see the cost. requests = guaranteed floor; limits = hard ceiling.

trust.description: C8: supply-chain trust block. OPTIONAL at the solo tier (unsigned local plugins are allowed); REQUIRED by team+ deployments (signing default-required — 'install on trust' is not a trust model). When present, the core verifies the signature against signed_by before install.

signature.description: Detached signature over the plugin image digest (cosign/Sigstore-style).

signed_by.description: Identity that signed the plugin (e.g. a Sigstore OIDC identity or key reference).

capabilityUri.description: A capability URI: rat://<axis>/<major-version>/<capability>. The ONLY coupling between plugins. Mirrors the proto package + service method coordinate (ADR-002 D2/D4).

revision.description: Q02 5a / ADR-017 — PROVIDER-side within-major behavioral revision. A capability URI pins only the MAJOR (rat://axis/vN/cap); behavior added additively INSIDE an existing method (new response fields/semantics — e.g. honoring the ADR-012 crash-safety fields already_applied/expected_rows) carries a monotonic revision. Set on `provides` to advertise the highest behavioral revision this plugin IMPLEMENTS for the capability. Default (absent) == revision 1 (the frozen-at-v1 behavior).

min_revision.description: Q02 5a / ADR-017 — REQUIRER-side floor. Set on `requires` to demand a provider that IMPLEMENTS at least this behavioral revision of the capability. The reconciler/gateway refuses a binding whose provider advertises a lower `revision` — graceful within-major negotiation without SemVer ranges, so a version-skewed pairing fails CLEANLY instead of silently (e.g. an idempotency-relying consumer never silently sees already_applied=false from an old provider). Absent == no floor (any revision of the major satisfies). The capability-URI-per-behavior convention remains the alternative for a hard behavioral split.
